page: Amazon Web Services m9g.12xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an ARMv9-based AWS Graviton processor with 48 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, utilizing the Neoverse-V3 architecture. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance type. Network connectivity is delivered via a 25 Gbps baseline interface.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core processing capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 96265.95 and a PassMark CPU score of 49297.39. Memory performance is highly efficient, registering a PassMark memory score in the top tier and a cached read-only memory bandwidth of 6846.75 GB/sec. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both achieve top-tier ratings, reaching 36.49 GB/sec and 624.06 GB/sec respectively. However, single-core CPU performance and large-block memory write speeds remain in the average tier.

The instance demonstrates exceptional efficiency in database operations and web serving, recording a top-tier PassMark database score of 46882.25 and 136.27 million Redis SET operations per second. Static web serving throughput is also highly optimized at 97.07 GB/sec. Given the dedicated ARMv9 core architecture and high memory bandwidth, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for scale-out workloads, containerized microservices, and high-throughput web servers, though it is less suited for workloads requiring local NVMe storage or hardware acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m9g.12xlarge is a general-purpose server instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an ARMv9-based AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz, paired with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core performance and top-tier memory bandwidth, alongside exceptional database and web serving capabilities, including 136.27 million Redis SET operations per second. Conversely, single-core performance and large-block memory write speeds fall into the average category. This hardware profile is highly efficient for database operations, multi-threaded decompression tasks, and static web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.12xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 server featuring 48 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V3 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for database and web workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9g.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server with 48 dedicated Graviton cores, 192 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and high-bandwidth memory for database and high-throughput web serving workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 ARM64 cores running at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Delivers 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Achieves top-tier performance in database operations and Redis SET transactions
Optimized for multi-threaded decompression and static web serving workloads
